visual_evidence_rule: local capture forbidden; acceptance requires cloud-hosted build plus Sense Simulation review

single_tank_review.asset_policy: one linked vanilla Sherman GLB; existing constrained albedo texture set linked at runtime; no copied model or texture variant
single_tank_review.acceptance: Sense Simulation must confirm one Sherman with visible olive armor albedo, visible tread albedo, no fused/parade/assay scene, and right-side camera interaction.

authored_boxmodel_review.uv_policy: box and planar UV plates, one 0-1 paintable plate per surface family; no atlas packing
authored_boxmodel_review.asset_policy: fully authored Blender box-model chassis with solidified overlapping armor plates, connected multi-material cast turret shell with no pasted turret panels, and coaxial MG; no Meshy chassis or turret imports; box UV PNG plates for DALL-E paintability

visual_verdict.expected_visible_relationship: The hosted boxmodel tank should visibly differ from the rejected build: one connected cast turret shell should replace pasted turret panels, roadwheel/hub/bogie band should visibly read outside the track slab, and lower hull/track cracks should look materially covered as joined armor.
visual_verdict.actual_visible_relationship: User reported no-op churn after v1-15: the visible tank did not materially improve enough to count as a visible delta.
did_not_change: No accepted cloud/Sense evidence shows a visible improvement for the turret-panel relationship.
No accepted cloud/Sense evidence shows a visible improvement for the wheel-band relationship.
No accepted cloud/Sense evidence shows the lower hull/track cracks visually closed as joined armor.
visual_verdict.diagnostics_assessment: Source, GLB, manifest, and hosted token checks changed, but those diagnostics contradict the user visual read and cannot prove visual success. The existing Blender visibility diagnostic was stale at v1-13 and cannot validate v1-15.
visual_verdict.acceptance_blocker: Requires fresh cloud/Sense verdict for this exact build token before any fixed/changed/ready-for-acceptance claim.
visual_verdict.next_allowed_work: Repair the evidence gate and visual-delta measurement before making another geometry tweak.

visual_repair_intake.visible_target: A single authored Sherman-family tank whose hull, turret, wheels, track wells, barrel, and coaxial MG read as coherent joined metal, with no visible no-op from prior rejected builds.
visual_repair_intake.current_actual_read: User reports v1-15 is no-op churn: source and tokens changed, but the visible tank did not materially improve.
visual_repair_intake.forbidden_old_mistake: Do not add small panels, plugs, blockers, pasted turret plates, source-token proofs, stale diagnostic renders, or browser wake as a substitute for visible relationship change.
visual_repair_intake.single_edit_class: Evidence-gated redesign or one named exterior surface repair only; no broad tank improvement pass and no multiple speculative geometry tweaks in one commit.
visual_repair_intake.accepted_evidence_needed: Cloud-hosted Sense Simulation verdict for the exact build token, naming expected relationship, actual relationship, what visibly changed, what did not change, and whether diagnostics agree or contradict the visual read.
visual_repair_intake.what_would_prove_no_op: A fresh cloud/Sense or user visual report says the visible silhouette, turret/wheel/gap relationship, or armor continuity is materially unchanged despite source, GLB, or token changes.
next_allowed_actions: Refresh diagnostic renders only to understand failure, not to claim success.
Choose repair versus replacement from visual evidence before editing geometry.
If repairing, alter the exterior armor surface itself rather than adding blockers or decorative panels.
If replacing, build fewer larger coherent metal volumes with Sherman-readable silhouette.
visual_repair_intake.dominant_shape_rule: Future geometry work must replace or materially reshape a dominant exterior form: hull/sponson shell, track slab/skirt volume, turret mass, or full track-well armor surface. Detail-only mesh changes are forbidden.
what_not_to_build: wheel-only readability tweaks while track slabs dominate

visible_failure_packet.visible_failure: The tank keeps reading as the same mesh because the dominant silhouette-driving forms did not materially change.
dominant_unchanged_shapes: hull_lower_tub__hull_left remains the same broad rectangular hull mass
left_sloped_sponson__hull_left and right_sloped_sponson__hull_right remain the same side silhouette drivers
left_track_motion and right_track_motion remain the same long dark track slab drivers
runtime camera, centering, lighting, material palette, and model rotation remain constant, so small detail changes are visually swallowed
hidden_or_subordinate_changed_shapes: roadwheel diameter and hub changes are subordinate to the long dark track slabs and skirts
turret panel removal changes internal/secondary turret construction but leaves a similar turret mass in the same camera read
slot-wall and shoulder pieces are too small and too local to dominate the visible hull/track gap read
visible_failure_packet.failed_visible_relationship: The exterior tank form should visibly change at the silhouette and armor-continuity level; instead only small local components changed while the viewer still sees the same hull, track, and turret read.
visible_failure_packet.why_prior_change_passed_diagnostics: Bbox, node, raycast, triangle-count, material-slot, and token checks proved that geometry existed or moved numerically, but none measured whether the silhouette-driving surfaces changed enough to be visible in the hosted review camera.
visible_failure_packet.next_geometry_rule: Do not build another mesh unless the edit replaces or materially reshapes a dominant exterior form: hull/sponson shell, track slab/skirt volume, turret mass, or full track-well armor surface.
visible_failure_packet.repair_vs_replacement_decision: Default to replacement of dominant exterior forms unless fresh diagnostic evidence isolates one named exterior surface whose replacement can visibly change the silhouette.
no_build_if: The proposed change only adds panels, plugs, blockers, small covers, hubs, or hidden/internal faces.
The proposed change leaves hull/sponson/track/turret dominant bboxes effectively unchanged.
The proposed proof relies on tokens, nodes, bboxes, raycasts, or stale diagnostic renders without cloud/Sense visible delta.

authored_boxmodel_review.acceptance: Sense Simulation must confirm Sherman silhouette, connected cast turret massing with no cheek/side/roof pasted panels, smaller integrated track-well slot walls plus joined sponson shells close the front-left, front-right, rear-left, and rear-right lower hull/track cracks as joined metal, pass targeted no-wing slot-wall, no-pasted-turret-panel, and readable wheel/hub/bogie checks, and crack rays from outside those visible gaps hit exterior armor before entering the tank interior, with no pasted panels, blockers, floating boxes, or runtime overlays, armor reads as joined metal rather than separated cardboard planes, barrel and coaxial MG belong to the mantlet, box UV texture plates map sanely, and local capture was not used.
authored_boxmodel_review.tuner_acceptance: Sense Simulation must review boxmodel-tank.html?tune=1 as a preserved future-use gesture-only boxmodel part tuner: collapsed parts drawer is usable, four hull-colored flat armor panels are available for front-right, front-left, rear-right, and rear-left track-line holes, one selected panel is highlighted for editing, already enabled panels remain visible, Move/Rotate/Scale are one active mode at a time, Scale exposes explicit All/X/Y/Z axis buttons, drag/pinch/twist gestures visibly change the selected panel, OrbitControls camera orbit/dolly/pan works, the camera orientation widget snaps square front/back/left/right/top views, tank and panels share the same unskewed model frame, no object transform handles appear, and local capture was not used.

authored_treads_review.component_scope: full tread assembly only: open perimeter tread sidewall frame, wheels inside the inner profile opening, sprockets, idlers, return rollers, bogie connectors, and connector mounts; no hull, turret, barrel, coaxial MG, full tank scene, or texture variant

profile.subdivision_layer: one added silhouette layer around returns and run transitions

authored_treads_review.asset_policy: new isolated Blender tread component; failed boxmodel and textureable full-tank exporters are red evidence only and are not source architecture for this pass
authored_treads_review.acceptance: Sense Simulation must confirm full tread assembly only: open perimeter sidewall frame with wheels inside the inner profile opening, sprockets, idlers, return rollers, bogie connectors, one silhouette subdivision layer beyond the old 8-point profile, connector mounts subordinate to belt mass, preserve OrbitControls camera and orientation widget, no rail/cage/box-pod read, no hull, turret, barrel, coaxial MG, or full tank scene, and local capture was not used.

authored_textureable_review.uv_policy: box and planar UV plates, one 0-1 paintable plate per surface family; no atlas packing
authored_textureable_review.asset_policy: new authored textureable base; failed boxmodel remains red evidence only; no Meshy chassis or turret imports; split UV plates and decal anchors prepare commander identity without geometry variants
authored_textureable_review.acceptance: Sense Simulation must confirm a usable textureable Sherman base: closed 3D track pods with top, bottom, front, rear, inner, and outer band thickness; wheels contained inside the track/skirt volume rather than pasted outside; upper skirt does not cut through wheel tops; side profile is not the failed distorted slab; turret ring gap hidden by turret lower overlap; integrated hatches sit in the turret roof rather than floating coins; barrel and coaxial MG belong to the mantlet/elevation pivot; split UV plates map sanely for paint/decal work; local capture was not used.

authored_retopo_review.uv_policy: split face texture plates, one 0-1 rectangular UV material per paintable surface group
authored_retopo_review.asset_policy: fully authored hard-surface chassis and turret; no Meshy chassis or turret imports; split face PNG plates for DALL-E paintability
authored_retopo_review.acceptance: Sense Simulation must confirm close-up chassis and turret read as usable hard-surface armor, barrel belongs to mantlet, split face texture plates map sanely, and local capture was not used.

tank_runtime.visual_truth_note: Fused Meshy GLB is the active visible non-toy tank body. It remains static for gameplay animation purposes because it has no separable turret, cannon, tread, wheel, or hatch nodes.

sherman_meshy_kit.visual_target: non-toy Sherman hard-surface kit: hull, traversable turret, elevatable cannon, repeated low-budget gear module, authored tread ribbon later

phone_budget.warning: RED BUILD: phone budget numbers are diagnostic only until human visual review accepts the GLB parts. Do not integrate or promote.

red_build.reason: Meshy parts were generated before the user visually reviewed and accepted the cloud GLB viewer. Cloud deploy plus termux-open-url did not secure attention or acceptance.
red_build.expected_attention_gate: wake user at selected source review and wait for explicit visual acceptance before Meshy spend; wake again at GLB review before any runtime integration claim
red_build.actual_failure: agent proceeded from generated source pack into Meshy submissions and reported capability without confirmed user visual review
red_build.preservation_policy: preserve downloaded GLB/FBX/provenance as red-build artifacts; do not wire into runtime or promote until human review accepts visible parts
red_build.required_recovery: fresh browser wake to cloud GLB viewer, explicit user acceptance or rejection per part, then update manifest before integration

geometry_failure.observed_by_user: turret and barrel diverge; models are not compatible
geometry_failure.cause: turret shell and mantlet/barrel were generated from separate source images without a shared socket profile, shared scale reference, common local origin, or explicit cannon elevation axis
geometry_failure.visual_truth: the mantlet/barrel does not read as mechanically belonging to the turret face
geometry_failure.perceptual_truth: the cannon appears as an unrelated part rather than an elevatable gun assembly owned by the turret
geometry_failure.behavioral_truth: cannot proceed to turret traversal/cannon verticality assembly from these two parts
geometry_failure.required_recovery: Keep the Meshy barrel-only asset. Regenerate or create a compatible turret/mantlet/socket asset that provides the rear barrel pivot and visual ownership; do not use the rejected overmodeled mantlet/barrel block.

animation_proof.runtime_strategy: shared geometry/material extraction plus InstancedMesh for 24-tank spawn proof; every spawned tank has independent drive, wheel, tread, turret, and barrel animation state; no deep-cloned GLB object trees
animation_proof.explicit_red_condition: human visual review must accept canonical kit body, cannon-chain MG with no fixed bow MG, closed 3D tread volume, wheel orientation, non-black rear-pivot barrel verticality, turret/barrel ownership, independent 24-tank motion, and phone performance from the cloud page
animation_proof.barrel_quality_gate: rejected_black_center_pivot_barrel; require visible rear-socket vertical elevation and non-black Sherman-compatible PBR material
animation_proof.canonical_acceptance_source: user accepted model-assay body and tread on 2026-07-04; this is the canonical tank, not the fused Alpha parade GLB

gear_hero_reference.role: higher-detail gear reference, not default phone runtime duplicate

barrel_only.role: low-poly cannon barrel visual tube; rear end must be parented to a future compatible mantlet/socket pivot

required_cloud_captures: treadfirst-treads phone portrait showing authored_sherman_treads_v1 and build token tftm-authored-sherman-treads-v1-3-20260705, with wheels/sprockets/idlers/rollers/bogie connectors occupying the inner profile opening and no hull/turret/full tank geometry

treadfirst-treads phone landscape showing full tread assembly showing an open perimeter sidewall frame, wheels/sprockets/idlers/return rollers/bogie arms occupying the inner profile opening, connector mounts subordinate, one silhouette subdivision layer beyond the old 8-point tread, preserved OrbitControls camera/orientation widget, no rail/cage/box-pod read, and no local capture
boxmodel-tank phone portrait showing authored_sherman_boxmodel_v1 and build token tftm-authored-sherman-boxmodel-v1-15-20260705

boxmodel-tank close-up review showing smaller integrated track-well slot-wall coverage at front-left, front-right, rear-left, and rear-right as attached armor, no raycast-accessible interior through those cracks, no side-wing silhouette deformation, solidified armor plates, barrel/mantlet/coaxial MG ownership, and box UV plate paintability

boxmodel-tank.html?tune=1 phone portrait showing gesture-only part tuner with collapsed parts drawer, front-right/front-left/rear-right/rear-left hull-colored flat armor panel parts aligned parallel to the tracks, flush to the side plane, and not protruding like blocks, selected panel highlight, camera orientation widget, explicit All/X/Y/Z scale axis buttons, square unskewed tank frame, and build token tftm-authored-sherman-boxmodel-tuner-v9-20260704

boxmodel-tank.html?tune=1 cloud interaction evidence showing one selected flat armor panel visibly move/rotate/scale through drag/pinch/twist while other enabled panels remain placed, OrbitControls camera orbit/dolly/pan preserved from an unskewed model frame, and no object transform handles
textureable-tank phone portrait showing authored_sherman_textureable_v1 and build token tftm-authored-sherman-textureable-v1-1-20260705

textureable-tank close-up review showing turret ring gap hidden, hatches integrated into turret roof, barrel and coaxial MG owned by mantlet/elevation pivot, and split UV plate paintability

model-assay phone landscape showing canonical body, cannon-chain MG, readable closed 3D tread belt volume, wheel orientation, and non-black barrel material
model-assay time-separated capture showing unsynchronized horizontal turret traverse, vertical barrel elevation, cannon-chain MG, and no fixed bow MG across the 24 tanks

model-assay desktop medium viewport showing FPS, draw-call estimate, and 24-tank budget readout

false_change_penalty.reason: Fresh cloud screenshots showed no visible delta after v1-8 front-gap coverage. v1-15 is also red by user report: source and GLB changed, but the visible relationship was no-op churn. Root causes include weak evidence gates that accepted source/token changes and stale diagnostic renders as progress.
false_change_penalty.required_next_evidence: Next tank visual pass must show cloud/Sense evidence that v1-15 cast-turret/readable-wheel armor visibly closes the front and rear lower hull/track cracks on all four corners without side-wing deformation and with visible enlarged roadwheel/hub/bogie band.

sense_simulation_questions: Does authored_sherman_treads_v1 show a full tread assembly only: open perimeter sidewall frame, wheels inside the inner profile opening, sprockets, idlers, return rollers, bogie connectors, and connector mounts, with no hull, turret, barrel, coaxial MG, or full tank scene?
Do the wheels occupy the empty inner tread profile opening in side view rather than exterior-plane space, hidden sidewall space, or an opaque side face?

Does the extra silhouette subdivision layer improve the old subdivision-0 tread profile without creating fused garbage or cardboard planes, and does the route preserve the established OrbitControls camera plus orientation widget?
Does authored_sherman_textureable_v1 build v1-1 show wheels contained inside closed track pods, with no skirt-through-wheel collision and no pasted coin wheels?
Does authored_sherman_textureable_v1 hide the turret ring gap, integrate the hatches into the turret roof, and preserve barrel/coaxial MG ownership by the mantlet/elevation pivot?
Do authored_sherman_textureable_v1 split UV plates map sanely enough to become the paint/decal base for Alpha and later commander identities?
Does authored_sherman_boxmodel_v1 build v1-15 preserve the Sherman silhouette while using smaller integrated slot walls across all four lower hull/track cracks?

On boxmodel-tank.html?tune=1, does the gesture-only parts workflow provide four hull-colored front-right/front-left/rear-right/rear-left flat armor panels aligned parallel to the tracks and reading like armor skin, not blocks while using a collapsed drawer, one active transform mode, explicit All/X/Y/Z scale axes, direct gestures, OrbitControls camera, and a camera orientation widget without object transform handles?
Does the turret read as one connected cast turret shell with cheek mass, roof flattening, rear bustle, and no pasted side/roof/front panels?

Do the smaller integrated slot walls and joined sponson shells bridge the hull side into the outer track skirt at front-left, front-right, rear-left, and rear-right lower cracks, visibly differ from the rejected screenshot, and do outside crack raycasts hit exterior armor before entering the tank interior, without pasted panels, blockers, or side wings?
Do the armor plates read as joined metal mass rather than separated cardboard planes?
Is the coaxial MG visible and owned by the mantlet/gun assembly?
Do the box UV plates stay paintable without obvious runtime guide seams or DALL-E-unpaintable UV spaghetti?
Does authored_sherman_retopo_v1 read as a usable hard-surface chassis and turret at close-up distance?
Do the split face texture plates map sanely without stretching, seams across the wrong surfaces, or DALL-E-unpaintable UV spaghetti?
Does each candidate read as Sherman hard-surface rather than toy, pillow, primitive assembly, or soft sculpture?
Are hull candidates actually hull shells without tracks, wheels, turret, barrel, or complete running gear?
Are turret candidates separate turret shells without hull, tracks, wheels, chassis, or long barrel?
Is the mantlet/barrel candidate isolated from turret body and hull?
Is the gear/wheel candidate a reusable wheel or sprocket module, not a belt or whole suspension?
Is the material atlas free of text, labels, logos, and complete vehicle objects?
acceptance: RED BUILD: Animated 24-tank runtime proof is deployed for human visual review. It passes only if this canonical body/tread system reads as the tank, has cannon-chain MG with no fixed bow MG, treads read as closed 3D tread belt volumes rather than side facades, wheels face the hull sides, barrels use non-black Sherman-compatible PBR material and visibly elevate from a rear/socket pivot, every turret traverses horizontally on unsynchronized smooth cycles, every tank has independent motion state, and 24 tanks are plausible on phone.
